differential_diagnoses: Meconium aspiration syndrome with air leak complications
Pulmonary hypoplasia associated with renal anomalies
Persistent pulmonary hypertension of the newborn
next_steps: Diagnostic bronchoscopy to rule out endobronchial obstruction or foreign body
Surgical consultation for persistent lung opacity and potential mass lesion
Serial monitoring of inflammatory markers (CRP, procalcitonin) to evaluate for necrotizing pneumonia
Genetic counseling and evaluation for syndromic associations related to urinary tract dilatation and pulmonary findings

differential_diagnoses: Post-infectious necrotizing pneumonia with persistent abscess or pneumatocele
Meconium aspiration syndrome with localized sequestration
next_steps: Contrast-enhanced chest computed tomography (CT) to define blood supply and anatomy of the focal opacity
Bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL) and protected specimen brush for deep airway cultures
Formal cardiothoracic surgery consultation for potential biopsy or excision
Renal ultrasound and voiding cystourethrogram (VCUG) to assess the urinary tract pathology
Echocardiographic assessment of the persistent pulmonary hypertension and potential vascular source (e.g., systemic arterial supply to the sequestration)
Genetic consultation for evaluation of syndromic associations given the renal and pulmonary findings
